questions.q: By which short name is Jayaprakash Narayan popularly known?
questions.explanation: Jayaprakash Narayan is popularly known as JP.

questions.q: Jayaprakash Narayan called for which idea during his mid-1970s movement?
questions.explanation: JP led a mass movement calling for 'Total Revolution.'

questions.q: In which decade did Jayaprakash Narayan lead his best-known mass movement?
questions.explanation: He led the movement in the mid-1970s.

questions.q: JP's movement is described as a focal point of which kind of politics during that period?
questions.explanation: His movement became a focal point of opposition politics during that period.

questions.q: Jayaprakash Narayan's movement is most closely associated with protests originating in which state?
questions.explanation: The movement is closely associated with Bihar, where student protests gave it momentum.

questions.q: JP's 'Total Revolution' call was primarily aimed at bringing about change in which sphere?
questions.explanation: Total Revolution called for wide-ranging change in society and the political system.

questions.q: Before his mid-1970s movement, JP had been associated with which broad political tradition?
questions.explanation: JP was long associated with the socialist tradition in Indian politics.

questions.q: JP's movement of the mid-1970s was led by him largely from outside which kind of office?
questions.explanation: JP led the movement as a public figure rather than from a formal governmental office.

questions.q: Jayaprakash Narayan's movement drew strong participation from which group?
questions.explanation: Students and youth were prominent participants in JP's movement.

questions.q: What is the commonly used full name behind the initials 'JP'?
questions.explanation: JP stands for Jayaprakash Narayan.

questions.q: In which year did JP's mass movement reach its peak before a major constitutional event in India?
questions.explanation: JP's movement reached its peak in 1974-75.

questions.q: JP's movement is widely regarded as a precursor to which 1977 development at the Union level?
options: The formation of the Janata Party government
questions.explanation: The movement is widely seen as a precursor to the Janata Party government formed in 1977.

questions.q: In his earlier political life, JP was a leading figure in which party founded in the 1930s?
questions.explanation: JP was a leading figure in the Congress Socialist Party, formed in the 1930s.

questions.q: Before his political activism turned to movements, JP was associated for a time with which constructive social effort?
questions.explanation: JP was associated for a period with the Bhoodan land-gift movement led by Vinoba Bhave.

questions.q: JP pursued higher education in which country during the 1920s?
questions.explanation: JP studied in the United States during the 1920s.

questions.q: JP was an active participant in which earlier nationwide movement of 1942?
questions.explanation: JP was an active participant in the Quit India Movement of 1942.

questions.q: The student-led agitation in Bihar that JP came to lead is sometimes referred to as the:
questions.explanation: The Bihar student agitation that JP led is often called the Bihar Movement.

questions.q: JP's 'Total Revolution' encompassed change across multiple dimensions; which one of these is commonly listed among them?
questions.explanation: Total Revolution was described as encompassing social, economic and political transformation.

questions.q: JP's broad approach to political change drew on the philosophy of which earlier figure he admired?
questions.explanation: JP's later thought drew heavily on Gandhian ideas of social and political change.

questions.q: Jayaprakash Narayan was posthumously conferred which of India's highest civilian honours?
questions.explanation: JP was posthumously awarded the Bharat Ratna, India's highest civilian honour.

questions.q: JP's emphasis on people's participation in governance is sometimes summed up by which concept he promoted?
questions.explanation: JP advocated 'Lokniti', a politics centred on the people rather than only on parties.

questions.q: JP renounced active party politics at one stage to focus on which kind of work?
questions.explanation: For a period JP withdrew from party politics to engage in Sarvodaya and constructive social work.

questions.q: In which year was Jayaprakash Narayan born?
questions.explanation: Jayaprakash Narayan was born in 1902.

questions.q: In which year did Jayaprakash Narayan die?
questions.explanation: JP died in 1979.

questions.q: JP's birthplace lies in which present-day Indian state, near the Bihar region he was associated with?
questions.explanation: JP was born in a village in the Bihar region with which he remained closely associated.

questions.q: JP is widely referred to by which honorific epithet in Hindi reflecting public esteem?
questions.explanation: JP is widely known by the epithet 'Lok Nayak', meaning 'leader of the people.'

questions.q: JP was associated with the founding of which socialist party in the years after independence?
questions.explanation: JP was associated with the formation of the Praja Socialist Party in the post-independence years.

questions.q: JP put forward proposals on Kashmir and on reconciliation; broadly, his approach favoured solutions based on what?
questions.explanation: JP generally advocated solutions grounded in dialogue and the consent of the people involved.

questions.q: JP's ideas on restructuring democracy emphasised strengthening institutions at which level?
questions.explanation: JP emphasised decentralised, participatory democracy rooted at the village and community level.

questions.q: A national institution and several public landmarks in India have been named in JP's honour chiefly to commemorate his role as a:
options: Leader of a major people's movement
questions.explanation: Institutions named after JP commemorate his role as the leader of a major people's movement.
